title: Monsoon Alert – Storm and Heavy Rain Alert in 14 States, Know Weather Forecast - Times Bull
article_text:
New Delhi: The heatwave has begun in most parts of India, with temperatures rising. May-like heat has already begun to plague people in March, causing discomfort. The heat is so intense that people are sweating profusely in the sun. A light fog enveloped Delhi-NCR and western Uttar Pradesh early in the morning.
Snowfall in Himachal Pradesh, Kashmir, and Uttarakhand has also caused temperatures to drop significantly, with temperatures below freezing in many areas. Higher temperatures are expected in Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Rajasthan. Details about the weather conditions can be found below.
The Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a rain warning for 14 states in the coming week. According to the IMD, rain is expected in Bihar, Jharkhand, West Bengal, and Odisha. In addition, Assam, Meghalaya, Nagaland, Manipur, and Mizoram have also issued rain alerts for thunderstorms and lightning.
Rain is also expected in Tripura, Arunachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh, and Jammu and Kashmir. According to the IMD, hailstorms and lightning are expected in many parts of Jammu and Kashmir and Himachal Pradesh on March 11 and 12. Winds of up to 50 kilometres per hour are also expected in many areas along with rain.
According to the IMD, cloudy skies are expected in Delhi from March 11. Cloudy skies and drizzle are expected until March 14. From March 15, temperatures will rise rapidly, and heat waves are expected. The maximum temperature in Delhi tomorrow is expected to be 36 degrees Celsius, and the minimum temperatureis  19 degrees Celsius.
The Meteorological Department has not predicted any rain in Uttar Pradesh on March 11. This means that a clear weather alert has been issued. Starting today, temperatures will begin to rise in more than 20 districts of Uttar Pradesh.
Temperatures may rise in Meerut, Baghpat, Hapur, Ghaziabad, Agra, and Mathura. Temperatures may also rise in Firozabad, Aligarh, Hathras, Moradabad, Amroha, Gorakhpur, Deoria, Prayagraj, Basti, and Fatehpur.
